standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use all pronouns, including intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves) correctly.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Spell correctly.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, connecting insights gained from specific details to an understanding of the text as a whole.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Know number names and the count sequence.
standards.standard_text: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Know number names and the count sequence.
standards.standard_text: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
standards.standard_text: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
standards.standard_text: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Graph linear and quadratic functions and show intercepts, maxima, and minima.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Graph square root, cube root, and piecewise-defined functions, including step functions and absolute value functions.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions
standards.cluster: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions
standards.standard_text: Understand that polynomials form a system analogous to the integers, namely, they are closed under the operations of addition, subtraction, and multiplication; add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions
standards.cluster: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions
standards.standard_text: Know and apply the Remainder Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number a, the remainder on division by x - a is p(a), so p(a) = 0 if and only if (x - a) is a factor of p(x).

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Build a function that models a relationship between two quantities
standards.standard_text: Determine an explicit expression, a recursive process, or steps for calculation from a context.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Build a function that models a relationship between two quantities
standards.standard_text: Combine standard function types using arithmetic operations.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Prove that all circles are similar.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Identify and describe relationships among inscribed angles, radii, and chords.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability
standards.cluster: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability
standards.standard_text: Describe events as subsets of a sample space (the set of outcomes) using characteristics (or categories) of the outcomes, or as unions, intersections, or complements of other events ("or," "and," "not").

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability
standards.cluster: Conditional Probability and the Rules of Probability
standards.standard_text: Understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
